s_spiffe.label: SPIFFE / SPIRE
s_spiffe.sublabel: workload identity · JWT SVID with audience claim

s_netns.label: Network Namespace (netns)
s_netns.sublabel: isolated namespace · no direct cluster access

s_iptables.label: iptables proxy-init
s_iptables.sublabel: traffic → Envoy · port 443 excluded for JWKS

s_seccomp.label: Seccomp
s_seccomp.sublabel: restricts system calls available to sandbox process

s_landlock.label: Landlock
s_landlock.sublabel: filesystem isolation · sandbox cannot access host paths

s_inherit.label: Subprocess Inheritance
s_inherit.sublabel: child processes inherit all restrictions · no exec escape

s_nosecrets.label: No Static Secrets
s_nosecrets.sublabel: SPIFFE SVID replaces pre-shared keys · auto-registered client

s_shortlived.label: Short-Lived Tokens
s_shortlived.sublabel: JWT 5-min TTL · SVID auto-rotated by spiffe-helper

s_accesstok.label: Access Token (not ID token)
s_accesstok.sublabel: real OAuth access token forwarded · correct audience for validation

sally.title: Sally (End User)
sally.description: An authenticated user who accesses data through the Hermes AI dashboard. Sally's identity is established via Keycloak OIDC login and carried as an RS256-signed JWT through the entire zero-trust chain.

keycloak.title: Keycloak (Identity Provider)
keycloak.description: OpenID Connect identity provider. Issues RS256-signed JWTs with a 5-minute TTL after user authentication. Publishes JWKS endpoint for downstream signature verification. Token audience is bound to the MCP service's SPIFFE identity.

hermes.title: Hermes Dashboard
hermes.description: AI agent dashboard that holds Sally's JWT after OIDC login. When the agent calls MCP tools, Hermes includes the access token in the Authorization header. Registered in Keycloak as the authorized party (azp).

svc.title: Kubernetes Service
svc.description: Cluster-internal Service object exposing the MCP pod on port 9090. All traffic enters the pod through this service, which routes to the Envoy sidecar.

envoy.title: Envoy Proxy (Istio Sidecar)
envoy.description: Istio-injected Envoy sidecar that intercepts all inbound traffic. Configured with ext_authz filter pointing to AuthBridge for JWT validation before forwarding to the MCP application.

authbridge.title: AuthBridge (JWT Validator)
authbridge.description: Sidecar service invoked by Envoy's ext_authz filter. Validates incoming RS256-signed JWTs by fetching the signing key (kid: UWDn_5Ogw...) from the Keycloak JWKS endpoint, verifying the SPIFFE audience claim, and extracting identity for downstream services.
details: RS256 verified via Keycloak JWKS (kid lookup)
iss must match Keycloak realm URL

mcp.title: MCP Server
mcp.description: Model Context Protocol server running the actual tool implementations. Reads the validated identity from headers injected by AuthBridge, then checks fine-grained permissions via SpiceDB before executing queries.

spicedb.title: SpiceDB (Authorization)
spicedb.description: Google Zanzibar-inspired authorization system. The MCP server calls CheckPermission to verify whether the authenticated user has access to the requested resource (dataset, catalog, etc.).

trino.title: Trino (Data Query Engine)
trino.description: Distributed SQL query engine. The MCP server executes Trino queries only after SpiceDB confirms the user has permission to access the requested dataset.

sec_identity.title: Identity & Authentication
sec_identity.description: Controls that establish and verify who is making the request. Defends against gateway bypass and unauthorized access.
details: Keycloak OIDC, SPIFFE/SPIRE, AuthBridge JWT, On-Behalf-Of

s_keycloak.title: Keycloak OIDC Login
s_keycloak.description: User authenticates via OpenID Connect and receives an RS256-signed JWT. The token carries the user's identity (preferred_username, sub, email) and is bound to the MCP service's SPIFFE audience.

s_spiffe.title: SPIFFE / SPIRE Workload Identity
s_spiffe.description: Provides cryptographic workload identity for the MCP pod. The JWT audience claim (spiffe://retail-demo/ns/openshell/sa/default) is the pod's SPIFFE ID, ensuring tokens can only be accepted by the intended service.
details: Binds JWT audience to workload identity

s_authbridge.title: AuthBridge JWT Validation
s_authbridge.description: Verifies every incoming JWT signature by fetching the signing key (kid: UWDn_5Ogw...) from Keycloak's JWKS endpoint. Checks that the audience claim matches the pod's SPIFFE identity. Runs on every request — fail-closed.

s_obo.title: On-Behalf-Of (JWT Audience)
s_obo.description: The access token carries Sally's identity end-to-end. The preferred_username claim identifies the user for SpiceDB checks, azp identifies the requesting client, and the SPIFFE audience ensures the token is only valid for this service.

sec_authz.title: Authorization
sec_authz.description: Controls that determine what an authenticated user can do. Fine-grained permission checks and sandbox endpoint restrictions.

s_spicedb.title: SpiceDB CheckPermission
s_spicedb.description: Relationship-based access control (ReBAC) inspired by Google Zanzibar. Every MCP tool call triggers a CheckPermission call: can user:sally view dataset:orders? Permission decisions are based on stored relationships, not roles.

s_opa.title: OPA Sandbox Policy
s_opa.description: Open Policy Agent controls which endpoints the MCP sandbox process can reach. The allow-list restricts outbound connections to only MCP, Trino, and Keycloak — preventing network exfiltration to arbitrary services.
details: All other cluster services, external networks

sec_network.title: Network Isolation
sec_network.description: Controls that restrict network access from the sandbox. Defense-in-depth against network exfiltration and gateway bypass.
details: Network exfiltration, gateway bypass, lateral movement
netns isolation, iptables redirect, K8s Service routing

s_netns.title: Network Namespace (netns)
s_netns.description: The sandbox runs in an isolated Linux network namespace with no direct access to the cluster network. All network traffic must go through the configured proxy path.

s_iptables.title: iptables proxy-init
s_iptables.description: The proxy-init container configures iptables rules to redirect all inbound traffic through the Envoy sidecar proxy. Port 443 is excluded to allow direct JWKS endpoint access for AuthBridge certificate validation.
details: Gateway bypass — traffic cannot skip Envoy

s_k8sroute.title: K8s Service Routing
s_k8sroute.description: The Kubernetes Service targetPort is set to 15124 (Envoy), not 8080 (MCP app). This ensures all traffic entering the pod goes through the Envoy sidecar and AuthBridge validation — there is no path to reach MCP directly.
details: Gateway bypass — Service → Envoy is mandatory

sec_process.title: Process & Filesystem Isolation
sec_process.description: Linux kernel security controls that restrict what the sandbox process can do at the OS level. Prevents filesystem escape and restricts system calls.
details: Filesystem escape, container breakout, privilege escalation

s_seccomp.title: Seccomp
s_seccomp.description: Restricts the system calls available to the sandbox process. Blocks dangerous syscalls that could be used for container escape or privilege escalation.

s_landlock.title: Landlock
s_landlock.description: Linux Security Module that provides filesystem isolation. The sandbox process cannot access host paths outside its designated working directory, preventing data exfiltration via the filesystem.
details: Filesystem access restricted to sandbox dir

s_inherit.title: Subprocess Inheritance
s_inherit.description: All security restrictions (seccomp, Landlock, netns) are automatically inherited by child processes. An agent cannot escape the sandbox by spawning a subprocess via exec — the child inherits the parent's restrictions.

sec_cred.title: Credential Protection
sec_cred.description: Controls that minimize credential exposure. No static secrets, short-lived auto-rotating tokens, and trust-on-first-use for TLS.
details: Credential leaks, token replay, key compromise
TOFU, no static secrets, short-lived tokens, access token forwarding

s_tofu.title: TOFU Hashing
s_tofu.description: Trust-On-First-Use for gateway TLS certificates. On first connection, the certificate fingerprint is pinned. Subsequent connections reject any certificate that doesn't match, defending against MITM attacks.

s_nosecrets.title: No Static Secrets
s_nosecrets.description: SPIFFE SVIDs replace pre-shared keys and static credentials. The Keycloak client is auto-registered. No secrets are baked into images, config maps, or environment variables.

s_shortlived.title: Short-Lived Tokens
s_shortlived.description: JWTs have a 5-minute TTL, limiting the window for token replay. SPIFFE SVIDs are auto-rotated by spiffe-helper. Short-lived credentials reduce the blast radius of any credential compromise.

s_accesstok.title: Access Token (not ID token)
s_accesstok.description: Hermes forwards the real OAuth access token, not the ID token. The access token has the correct audience for AuthBridge validation (spiffe://retail-demo/...) and is designed for service-to-service authorization.
details: ID token audience is the client, not the service

steps.text: 1. Sally authenticates with Keycloak
2. Keycloak issues RS256 JWT (aud: spiffe://retail-demo/...)
⬡ Credential: RS256 JWT with 5-min TTL issued
⬡ Credential: Short-lived JWT — 5-min TTL limits exposure
⬡ Credential: Access token (not ID token) forwarded
3. Agent calls MCP tool (JWT in Authorization: Bearer header)
⬡ Network: Service:9090 → Envoy:15124 routing enforced
⬡ Credential: TOFU — gateway TLS certificate pinned
⬡ Network: Sandbox in isolated network namespace
⬡ Process: Landlock filesystem isolation active
⬡ Process: Subprocess restrictions inherited by children
⬡ Authorization: OPA sandbox policy — endpoint allow-list
4. K8s Service routes to Envoy sidecar
⬡ Network: iptables redirects all traffic through Envoy
5. Envoy → AuthBridge (ext_authz check)
⬡ Identity: AuthBridge JWKS signature + audience check
⬡ Credential: No static secrets — SPIFFE SVID replaces keys
6. AuthBridge: RS256 verify via JWKS (kid: UWDn_5Ogw...)
⬡ Identity: SPIFFE audience matches workload identity
7. JWT valid: sub=53373b46..., azp=hermes-dashboard, aud=spiffe://...
⬡ Identity: On-Behalf-Of — preferred_username:sally forwarded
8. Identity injected: x-auth-username: sally, x-auth-email: sally@redhat.com
9. MCP reads preferred_username: sally from validated headers
10. SpiceDB: CheckPermission(user:sally, dataset:orders)
⬡ Authorization: SpiceDB ReBAC permission check
11. Permission: ALLOWED
12. MCP executes Trino query
⬡ Process: seccomp + Landlock restrictions active
13. Query results returned
14. Response flows back through mesh
15. Results displayed to Sally
1. Sally authenticates with Keycloak
2. Keycloak issues RS256 JWT (aud: spiffe://retail-demo/...)
⬡ Credential: RS256 JWT with 5-min TTL issued
⬡ Credential: Short-lived JWT — 5-min TTL limits exposure
⬡ Credential: Access token (not ID token) forwarded
3. Agent calls MCP tool (JWT in Authorization: Bearer header)
⬡ Network: Service:9090 → Envoy:15124 routing enforced
⬡ Credential: TOFU — gateway TLS certificate pinned
⬡ Network: Sandbox in isolated network namespace
⬡ Process: Landlock filesystem isolation active
⬡ Process: Subprocess restrictions inherited by children
⬡ Authorization: OPA sandbox policy — endpoint allow-list
4. K8s Service routes to Envoy sidecar
⬡ Network: iptables redirects all traffic through Envoy
5. Envoy → AuthBridge (ext_authz check)
⬡ Identity: AuthBridge JWKS signature + audience check
⬡ Credential: No static secrets — SPIFFE SVID replaces keys
6. AuthBridge: RS256 verify via JWKS (kid: UWDn_5Ogw...)
⬡ Identity: SPIFFE audience matches workload identity
7. JWT valid: sub=53373b46..., azp=hermes-dashboard, aud=spiffe://...
⬡ Identity: On-Behalf-Of — preferred_username:sally forwarded
8. Identity injected: x-auth-username: sally, x-auth-email: sally@redhat.com
9. MCP reads preferred_username: sally from validated headers
10. SpiceDB: CheckPermission(user:sally, catalog:finance)
⬡ Authorization: SpiceDB ReBAC permission check
11. Permission: DENIED
12. Access denied → MCP returns error
13. Error response through mesh
14. Permission denied shown to Sally
1. Request sent without Authorization header
⬡ Network: Service:9090 → Envoy:15124 routing enforced
⬡ Network: Sandbox in isolated network namespace
⬡ Process: Landlock filesystem isolation active
⬡ Process: Subprocess restrictions inherited by children
2. K8s Service routes to Envoy sidecar
⬡ Network: iptables redirects all traffic through Envoy
3. Envoy → AuthBridge (ext_authz check)
⬡ Identity: AuthBridge — no JWT present, fail-closed
4. AuthBridge: missing Authorization header
5. AuthBridge returns 401 to Envoy
6. Envoy rejects request
7. 401 Unauthorized → Hermes

happy.label: ③ MCP tool call with JWT
⑥ AuthBridge: JWKS signature check (kid lookup)
⑦ JWT signature valid, claims verified ✓
⑧ Request → MCP Server (identity in headers)

denied.label: ③ MCP tool call with JWT in Authorization header
⑥ AuthBridge: RS256 signature + claims verified
⑦ JWT signature valid, claims verified ✓
⑧ Request → MCP Server (identity in headers)
⑫ Access denied → MCP returns error
⑭ Permission denied shown to Sally
